decision_summary: Constraints are satisfied with a significant margin (58.6°C vs 85.0°C limit). Following the strategy shift suggestion to prefer material tuning as geometry changes hit limits, I will slightly increase the heat spreader conductivity to further minimize the chip temperature objective.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the peak temperature in the chip region while staying within the allowed 2.0x step ratio.

expected_effects: Improved thermal uniformity across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur as conductivity increases beyond current high values
Material property must remain physically realistic for the selected spreader material class
